sentences.base_language: Learning to make dumplings
sentences.target_language: 学包饺子
sentences.romanized: xué bāo jiǎozi
sentences.grammarNote: **学** (xué) = to learn. **包** (bāo) = to wrap/fold (used specifically for making dumplings, wontons, and similar wrapped foods). **饺子** (jiǎozi) = dumplings: **饺** (jiǎo, a kind of boiled dumpling) + **子** (zi, nominalizing suffix). The verb **包饺子** = 'wrap dumplings' — in Chinese, the making of dumplings is described as wrapping, not making.

sentences.base_language: My grandmother is the best dumpling maker in our family
sentences.target_language: 我奶奶是我们家包饺子包得最好的人
sentences.romanized: wǒ nǎinai shì wǒmen jiā bāo jiǎozi bāo de zuì hǎo de rén
sentences.grammarNote: **包饺子包得最好** = wraps dumplings the best. When a verb-object compound (like **包饺子**) has a degree complement, the verb is repeated: V+O + V + **得** + degree. **得** (de) links the verb to its evaluative result: **包得好** (wraps well), **说得快** (speaks fast). **奶奶** (nǎinai) = paternal grandmother. Chinese has distinct words for each grandparent: **爷爷** (yéye, paternal grandfather), **姥姥** (lǎolao, maternal grandmother), **姥爷** (lǎoye, maternal grandfather).

sentences.base_language: I always thought making dumplings was easy
sentences.target_language: 我一直以为包饺子很容易
sentences.romanized: wǒ yīzhí yǐwéi bāo jiǎozi hěn róngyì
sentences.grammarNote: **以为** (yǐwéi) = to think/believe (often implying the belief turned out to be wrong). Contrast **认为** (rènwéi) = to think/consider (neutral opinion). **我以为…** = 'I thought… (but I was wrong).' **一直** (yīzhí) = always/all along. **容易** (róngyì) = easy: **容** (contain/tolerate) + **易** (easy/change). Opposite: **困难** (kùnnan, difficult).

sentences.base_language: Until the day I tried it myself
sentences.target_language: 直到有一天我自己尝试了
sentences.romanized: zhídào yǒu yī tiān wǒ zìjǐ chángshì le
sentences.grammarNote: **直到** (zhídào) = until/up to the point that. **有一天** = one day (there was a day). **自己** (zìjǐ) = oneself/by oneself — a reflexive/emphatic pronoun: **我自己** = I myself. **尝试** (chángshì) = to try/attempt: **尝** (taste/try) + **试** (test). The **了** here marks the event as having occurred (a change happened).

sentences.base_language: Grandmother first taught me how to make the dough
sentences.target_language: 奶奶先教我怎么和面
sentences.romanized: nǎinai xiān jiāo wǒ zěnme huómiàn
sentences.grammarNote: **教** (jiāo) = to teach. **教我怎么…** = 'teach me how to…' — **怎么** (zěnme) in indirect questions means 'how.' **和面** (huómiàn) = to knead dough: **和/揉** (huo/róu, to mix/work) + **面** (miàn, flour/dough). Note: **和** here is read **huó** (2nd tone) in this context, not **hé** (and). This tonal distinction is important.

sentences.base_language: She said you have to knead the dough until it becomes completely smooth
sentences.target_language: 她说要把面团揉到完全光滑为止
sentences.romanized: tā shuō yào bǎ miàntuán róu dào wánquán guānghuá wéizhǐ
sentences.grammarNote: **把面团揉到…为止** = 'knead the dough until…' — **把** construction with **到…为止** (until). **为止** (wéizhǐ) = until/up to this point (marks the end condition). **揉** (róu) = to knead/rub. **光滑** (guānghuá) = smooth: **光** (shiny/smooth) + **滑** (slippery/smooth). **完全** (wánquán) = completely/entirely.

sentences.base_language: I kneaded for ten minutes but still couldn't get it smooth
sentences.target_language: 我揉了十分钟但还是揉不光滑
sentences.romanized: wǒ róu le shí fēnzhōng dàn háishi róu bù guānghuá
sentences.grammarNote: **还是** (háishi) = still/yet (indicating the situation hasn't changed despite effort). **揉不光滑** = couldn't knead it smooth: **揉** + **不** + **光滑** is a potential complement in the negative form — verb + **不** + result = 'unable to achieve the result.' Compare the positive form: **揉得光滑** (can get it smooth / managed to get it smooth).

sentences.base_language: Grandmother laughed and told me I needed to use more strength
sentences.target_language: 奶奶笑着告诉我要用更多的力气
sentences.romanized: nǎinai xiào zhe gàosu wǒ yào yòng gèng duō de lìqi
sentences.grammarNote: **笑着** (xiàozhe) = while laughing/with a laugh — **着** (zhe) describes a background/simultaneous state while the main action happens. **告诉** (gàosu) = to tell: **告** (announce) + **诉** (tell/narrate). **力气** (lìqi) = physical strength/effort: **力** (force) + **气** (energy/spirit). **更多** = even more/more: **更** (gèng) marks a comparison or increase.

sentences.base_language: She held my hands and showed me the correct technique
sentences.target_language: 她握着我的手，示范给我看正确的手法
sentences.romanized: tā wò zhe wǒ de shǒu, shìfàn gěi wǒ kàn zhèngquè de shǒufǎ
sentences.grammarNote: **握着** (wòzhe) = holding (ongoing) — **着** marks the sustained grip. **示范** (shìfàn) = to demonstrate/show by example: **示** (show) + **范** (model/example). **给我看** = show me (let me see): **给** + person + **看** is a common structure for 'show someone.' **手法** (shǒufǎ) = technique/method involving the hands: **手** (hand) + **法** (method). **正确** (zhèngquè) = correct/accurate.

sentences.base_language: Next came the hardest part — wrapping the dumplings
sentences.target_language: 接下来是最难的部分——包饺子
sentences.romanized: jiē xiàlái shì zuì nán de bùfen——bāo jiǎozi
sentences.grammarNote: **接下来** (jiē xiàlái) = next/coming up: **接** (receive/connect) + **下来** (come down/continue from here). A narrative transition phrase. **最难的部分** = the hardest part: **最** (superlative) + **难** (difficult) + **的** + **部分** (part/section). **难** (nán) = difficult, also: 难吃 (bad-tasting), 难看 (ugly), 难过 (feel bad/sad) — **难** + verb gives a negative quality.

sentences.base_language: You have to put the filling in the center of the wrapper
sentences.target_language: 要把馅儿放在饺子皮的正中间
sentences.romanized: yào bǎ xiànr fàng zài jiǎozi pí de zhèng zhōngjiān
sentences.grammarNote: **把馅儿放在…** = 'put the filling in/at…' — **把** construction: **把** + object (馅儿) + verb + location. **馅儿** (xiànr) = filling/stuffing: the **儿** (er) retroflexed suffix is typical of Beijing dialect. **饺子皮** (jiǎozi pí) = dumpling wrapper/skin: **皮** (pí) = skin. **正中间** (zhèng zhōngjiān) = exactly in the center: **正** (precisely/exactly) + **中间** (middle).

sentences.base_language: Then fold the wrapper in half and pinch the edges tightly
sentences.target_language: 然后把皮对折，把边捏紧
sentences.romanized: ránhòu bǎ pí duìzhé, bǎ biān niē jǐn
sentences.grammarNote: Two **把** constructions in sequence: **把皮对折** (fold the wrapper in half) + **把边捏紧** (pinch the edges tight). **对折** (duìzhé) = fold in half: **对** (facing/matching) + **折** (fold). **捏** (niē) = to pinch/squeeze (with fingers). **紧** (jǐn) = tight/firmly — a result complement: **捏紧** = pinch tight/firmly. This **把** + verb + result pattern is essential for instructions.

sentences.base_language: My first dumpling fell apart as soon as I picked it up
sentences.target_language: 我包的第一个饺子一拿起来就散了
sentences.romanized: wǒ bāo de dì yī gè jiǎozi yī ná qǐlái jiù sàn le
sentences.grammarNote: **一…就…** (yī…jiù…) = 'as soon as…': **一拿起来就散了** = 'as soon as I picked it up, it fell apart.' This is one of the most useful patterns in Chinese for 'immediately upon.' **拿起来** = pick up: **拿** (take/hold) + **起来** (up, directional). **散** (sàn) = fall apart/scatter. **我包的** = the one I wrapped — verb phrase + **的** modifies the noun.

sentences.base_language: Grandmother said it didn't matter, everyone fails the first time
sentences.target_language: 奶奶说没关系，每个人第一次都会失败的
sentences.romanized: nǎinai shuō méi guānxi, měi gè rén dì yī cì dōu huì shībài de
sentences.grammarNote: **没关系** (méi guānxi) = it doesn't matter / no worries. **每个人…都** = everyone…: **每** (every) + **个人** (person) + **都** (all/every, before verb). **会** (huì) here is predictive 'will': **都会失败** = will all fail. The sentence-final **的** adds a reassuring, matter-of-fact tone: '…it's just how things are.' **失败** (shībài) = to fail: opposite of **成功** (chénggōng, succeed).

sentences.base_language: After wrapping twenty dumplings my technique began to improve
sentences.target_language: 包了二十个之后，我的手法开始有所进步
sentences.romanized: bāo le èrshí gè zhīhòu, wǒ de shǒufǎ kāishǐ yǒu suǒ jìnbù
sentences.grammarNote: **包了二十个之后** = after having wrapped twenty: verb + **了** + quantity + **之后** (after). **有所进步** (yǒu suǒ jìnbù) = have made some improvement: **有所** + verb/noun = 'have made some [change].' This is a set literary pattern: **有所改善** (improved somewhat), **有所不同** (somewhat different). More formal than **有点进步**.

sentences.base_language: Grandmother said my dumplings still weren't pretty but at least they wouldn't fall apart
sentences.target_language: 奶奶说我包的饺子虽然不好看，但至少不会散了
sentences.romanized: nǎinai shuō wǒ bāo de jiǎozi suīrán bù hǎokàn, dàn zhìshǎo bù huì sàn le
sentences.grammarNote: **虽然…但…** (suīrán…dàn…) = although…but… — a concessive connector. Chinese typically uses both halves together (unlike English where 'although' and 'but' are not used together). **好看** (hǎokàn) = good-looking/pretty — the **好 + verb** pattern applied to appearance. **至少** (zhìshǎo) = at least. **不会散了** = won't fall apart anymore: **会** (predictive future) + **散** (scatter/come apart) + **了** (change of state).

sentences.base_language: We put all the dumplings into the boiling water together
sentences.target_language: 我们一起把所有的饺子放进了沸腾的水里
sentences.romanized: wǒmen yīqǐ bǎ suǒyǒu de jiǎozi fàng jìn le fèiténg de shuǐ lǐ
sentences.grammarNote: **把所有的饺子放进了…里** = 'put all the dumplings into…' — **把** construction with **进** (into) + **了** (completed). **放进** = put in/into: **放** (place/put) + **进** (enter/into). **沸腾** (fèiténg) = boiling/bubbling: **沸** (boil) + **腾** (surge/rise). More vivid than just **开水** (boiling water). **一起** = together — placed before the verb.

sentences.base_language: After they float to the surface they need to cook for another three minutes
sentences.target_language: 饺子浮起来之后还要再煮三分钟
sentences.romanized: jiǎozi fú qǐlái zhīhòu hái yào zài zhǔ sān fēnzhōng
sentences.grammarNote: **浮起来** (fú qǐlái) = float up to the surface: **浮** (float) + **起来** (up, directional complement). **起来** describes upward motion or emergence. **之后** = after. **还要再煮** = still need to cook for a further [time]: **还要** (still need to) + **再** (another/more) + **煮** (boil). The **还…再** combination emphasizes 'yet more' time needed.

sentences.base_language: When we scooped them out and put them on the plate I was very proud
sentences.target_language: 把饺子捞出来放在盘子里的时候，我非常自豪
sentences.romanized: bǎ jiǎozi lāo chūlái fàng zài pánzi lǐ de shíhou, wǒ fēicháng zìháo
sentences.grammarNote: **捞出来** (lāo chūlái) = scoop out: **捞** (lāo, fish out/scoop from liquid) + **出来** (come out, directional complement). **捞** is specifically used for scooping food out of water or liquid — a key cooking verb. **盘子** (pánzi) = plate/dish. **自豪** (zìháo) = proud (in a good sense): **自** (self) + **豪** (heroic/magnificent). Compare **骄傲** (jiāo'ào, arrogant/proud — can be positive or negative).

sentences.base_language: I asked grandmother whether she thought I would ever be able to make them as well as her
sentences.target_language: 我问奶奶，她觉得我以后能不能包得像她一样好
sentences.romanized: wǒ wèn nǎinai, tā juéde wǒ yǐhòu néng bù néng bāo de xiàng tā yīyàng hǎo
sentences.grammarNote: **能不能** = A-not-A question form for 'can or cannot': 'do you think I'll be able to?' **包得像她一样好** = wrap as well as her: **包得** + **像…一样好** = 'wrap to the extent of being as good as…' The **像…一样** (xiàng…yīyàng) = 'as…as…' comparison pattern. **以后** (yǐhòu) = in the future / someday. **觉得** = to think/feel (an opinion).

sentences.base_language: She smiled and said as long as I kept practicing I would definitely surpass her
sentences.target_language: 她笑着说，只要我坚持练习，一定会超过她的
sentences.romanized: tā xiào zhe shuō, zhǐyào wǒ jiānchí liànxí, yīdìng huì chāoguò tā de
sentences.grammarNote: **只要…一定…** = as long as… definitely… — a confident conditional: 'as long as you keep at it, you'll certainly.' **坚持** (jiānchí) = to persist/keep at it: **坚** (firm) + **持** (hold). **练习** (liànxí) = practice: **练** (drill/practise) + **习** (study/practise). **超过** (chāoguò) = to surpass/exceed: **超** (exceed) + **过** (pass). The sentence-final **的** adds reassuring certainty.

sentences.base_language: From then on I practiced making dumplings every time I went home
sentences.target_language: 从那以后，我每次回家都学着包饺子
sentences.romanized: cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ měi cì huí jiā dōu xué zhe bāo jiǎozi
sentences.grammarNote: **从那以后** (cóng nà yǐhòu) = from then on/ever since: **从** (from) + **那** (that time) + **以后** (afterward). A key narrative time phrase for marking a turning point. **每次…都** = every time…: **每次回家都** = every time (I) go home, (I) always… **学着** (xuézhe) = learning while doing — **着** here marks an ongoing attempt or process.

sentences.base_language: I still haven't surpassed my grandmother, but I'm getting closer and closer
sentences.target_language: 我还没有超过奶奶，但是越来越接近了
sentences.romanized: wǒ hái méiyǒu chāoguò nǎinai, dànshì yuèláiyuè jìjìn le
sentences.grammarNote: **还没有** (hái méiyǒu) = still haven't — **还** (still/yet) + **没有** (haven't). **越来越** (yuèláiyuè) = more and more / increasingly: **越来越** + adjective or verb = 'getting more and more [adj].' This is one of the most useful patterns in Chinese. **接近** (jìjìn) = to be close/approach: **接** (approach) + **近** (near). The final **了** marks a change of state — the getting-closer is happening now.
